title: What is a Vision Transformer?
concept: A Vision Transformer (ViT) applies the self-attention mechanism from natural language processing directly to images, representing a major shift away from traditional convolutional neural networks.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What makes test-time augmentation (TTA) computationally expensive?

options.text: It requires running multiple forward passes on augmented versions of the same image.
It permanently alters the model's weight matrices during testing.
It requires downloading new validation folders from the cloud.

exercises.explain: Correct! TTA increases accuracy by averaging predictions, but at the cost of running the model multiple times.

cards.title: A new eye
cards.body: For a decade, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) were the undisputed kings of computer vision, scanning images pixel-by-pixel with local filters. But in 2020, researchers wondered: what if we treated parts of an image like words in a sentence and used the same self-attention mechanism that powers models like ChatGPT?

cards.title: Vision Transformer (ViT)
cards.body: A Vision Transformer (ViT) is a deep learning architecture that processes images by dividing them into a sequence of patches, projecting those patches into embeddings, and using self-attention to model relationships across the entire image at once.

cards.title: Reading a puzzle
cards.body: A CNN is like looking at a puzzle through a tiny magnifying glass, sliding it slowly to see how adjacent pieces fit. A Vision Transformer is like spreading all the puzzle pieces on a table, looking at all of them simultaneously, and using attention to link pieces that match, regardless of how far apart they are.

cards.title: Global context
cards.body: When identifying a baseball player, a CNN must build up features from local edges to hands and bats across many layers. A ViT can immediately associate the texture of a glove on the left with the baseball on the far right in its very first layer using self-attention.

cards.title: No more convolutions!
cards.body: You might think ViT is just a fancy wrapper around CNNs. In fact, a pure Vision Transformer contains no traditional convolutional layers at all. It relies entirely on linear projections and self-attention to process spatial features.

cards.title: CNN vs. ViT Processing
cards.body: Comparing how CNNs and ViTs approach spatial image data:

left.text:
Locality-based filters
Gradually expands receptive field
Inherent translation invariance

right.text:
Sequence-based patches
Immediate global receptive field
Learns spatial relationships from scratch

exercises.prompt: What is the core mechanism that a Vision Transformer (ViT) uses to connect different parts of an image?
exercises.explain: ViTs apply self-attention across image patch sequences to capture relationships across the entire image.

exercises.prompt: True or false: A pure Vision Transformer (ViT) does not contain traditional convolutional layers.
exercises.explain: True. ViTs process images by flattening patches and routing them through a standard Transformer encoder without convolutions.
Self-attention allows ViTs to establish relationships between all parts of an image simultaneously.
exercises.sentence: While CNNs process images locally using sliding filters, ViTs process images globally using ___.

exercises.prompt: Which of these designs represents the input preparation for a Vision Transformer?
exercises.explain: ViTs prepare inputs by breaking the image into a grid of flattened patches, treating them like words in a sentence.

options.text: Splitting an image into a grid of small square patches and flattening them into vectors
Converting the image to grayscale and running a Sobel edge filter
Resizing the image to 1x1 to extract a single average color value

exercises.prompt: Why did researchers adapt the Transformer architecture (originally designed for NLP) to computer vision?

options.text: To capture global context immediately and bypass the local receptive field limitations of CNNs.
Because Transformers require much less memory than CNNs on small datasets.
To force models to use recurrent loops for temporal tracking.
To prevent the model from learning edge and texture filters.

exercises.explain: Excellent! By utilizing self-attention, ViT models can immediately capture global relationships across the entire image in the very first layer. Let's see how we prepare images for this transformer next!
